meaning: 爱 (ài) is the core word for love — romantic, familial, or compassionate alike. It forms 爱情 (romantic love), 可爱 ("lovable," i.e. cute), and 喜爱 (to be fond of).
note: One of the most requested characters for tattoos and calligraphy.

meaning: 和平 (hépíng) joins 和 (harmony) and 平 (calm, level) — peace between people and nations. The single character 和 alone carries the sense of harmony.
力量 (lìliàng) means strength and power, both physical and inner. The single character 力 means "force" or "effort."
勇气 (yǒngqì) combines 勇 (brave) and 气 (spirit, energy) — the courage to act in spite of fear.
智慧 (zhìhuì) is deep wisdom and insight, a step beyond mere knowledge (知识).
自由 (zìyóu) joins 自 (self) and 由 (to follow from) — to act from oneself; freedom and liberty.
幸福 (xìngfú) is deep, lasting happiness and well-being, distinct from momentary joy (快乐).
梦想 (mèngxiǎng) joins 梦 (dream) and 想 (to wish) — an aspiration or cherished dream, not the dream of sleep (梦).
希望 (xīwàng) means hope — both the feeling and the thing hoped for.
美 (měi) means beauty and the beautiful; it appears in 美丽 (beautiful) and 美好 (wonderful).
和谐 (héxié) means harmony and balance among people and things — a central ideal in Chinese thought.
忠诚 (zhōngchéng) means faithfulness and devotion, especially to people and principles.
荣誉 (róngyù) means honor and glory earned through one's deeds.
感恩 (gǎn'ēn) is heartfelt gratitude — literally "to feel kindness."
耐心 (nàixīn) joins 耐 (to endure) and 心 (heart) — patience as an enduring heart.
宁静 (níngjìng) means calm and tranquility — inner stillness.
真理 (zhēnlǐ) means truth in the deep, philosophical sense; everyday "true" is 真 (zhēn).
龙 (lóng) is the Chinese dragon — a benevolent symbol of power, luck, and the emperor, quite unlike the Western dragon.
note: Among the most popular characters for tattoos.

meaning: 虎 (hǔ) is the tiger, a symbol of courage and a guardian against evil.
凤凰 (fènghuáng) is the Chinese phoenix — a symbol of rebirth and grace, often paired with the dragon as the empress to its emperor.
狼 (láng) is the wolf, associated with loyalty to the pack and untamed independence.
莲花 (liánhuā) is the lotus — a Buddhist symbol of purity rising unstained from muddy water.
竹 (zhú) is bamboo, a symbol of resilience and integrity — it bends in the storm but does not break.
月亮 (yuèliang) is the moon; the single character 月 also means "month."
太阳 (tàiyáng) is the sun; the character 阳 alone is the "yang" of yin-yang.
水 (shuǐ) is water — one of the Five Elements (五行) of Chinese cosmology.
火 (huǒ) is fire — one of the Five Elements (五行).
山 (shān) is a mountain; the character is a pictograph of three peaks.
海 (hǎi) is the sea or ocean; it also appears in many place names.
雪 (xuě) is snow — a frequent image in classical Chinese poetry.
家 (jiā) means both family and home; the character shows a roof over a pig — the traditional household.
朋友 (péngyou) means friend; both 朋 and 友 historically meant companion.
母亲 (mǔqīn) is the formal word for mother; the affectionate form is 妈妈 (māma).
父亲 (fùqīn) is the formal word for father; the affectionate form is 爸爸 (bàba).
战士 (zhànshì) is a warrior or fighter — literally a "battle soldier."
王 (wáng) means king; it is also the single most common Chinese surname.
女王 (nǚwáng) is a reigning queen — 女 (woman) plus 王 (king).
幸运 (xìngyùn) means good luck and fortune; everyday "luck" is also 运气 (yùnqi).
成功 (chénggōng) means success — 成 (to accomplish) plus 功 (achievement).
永恒 (yǒnghéng) means eternity, the everlasting; 永 alone means "forever."
命运 (mìngyùn) means destiny and fate — 命 (life, fate) plus 运 (fortune, movement).
灵魂 (línghún) means the soul or spirit.
心 (xīn) is the heart — both the organ and the seat of emotion and mind in Chinese thought.
生命 (shēngmìng) is life in the vital sense; 生 alone means "to be born" or "to live."
精神 (jīngshén) means spirit, vigor, and mental energy.
福 (fú) means blessing and good fortune; it is the character hung — often upside-down — at Chinese New Year.
气 (qì) is qi — the vital life energy at the heart of Chinese philosophy, medicine, and martial arts.
